quotes.text: Something hit me very hard once, thinking about what one little man could do. Think of it. What one little man could do.
The nitrogen in our DNA, the calcium in our teeth, the iron in our blood, the carbon in our apple pies were made in the interiors of collapsing stars. We are made of starstuff.
Dad, look what I builded!
Tell me, what is it you plan to do with your one wild and precious life?
With the right context I'm an absolute genius.
When I am silent, I fall into the place where everything is music.
There is nothing in a caterpillar that tells you it's going to be a butterfly.

You do not need to leave your room. Remain sitting at your table and listen. Do not even listen, simply wait. Do not even wait, be quite still and solitary. The world will freely offer itself to you to be unmasked; it has no choice, it will roll in ecstasy at your feet.
The most important thing about art is to work. Nothing else matters except sitting down every day and trying.
I have nature and art and poetry, and if that is not enough, what is enough?
We shall not cease from exploration, and the end of all our exploring will be to arrive where we started and know the place for the first time.
The trim tab is the tiny thing on the rudder that turns the whole ship. That's what I am — a trim tab.
You cannot step twice into the same river.
The good life is one inspired by love and guided by knowledge.
Attention is the rarest and purest form of generosity.
I am large, I contain multitudes.
The universe is under no obligation to make sense to you.
We are a way for the cosmos to know itself.
Do I contradict myself? Very well then I contradict myself.
The opposite of a correct statement is a false statement. But the opposite of a profound truth may well be another profound truth.
Not everything that is faced can be changed, but nothing can be changed until it is faced.
The only way to make sense out of change is to plunge into it, move with it, and join the dance.
We are here to help each other get through this thing, whatever it is.
The wound is the place where the Light enters you.
You are the sky. Everything else — it's just the weather.
The most beautiful thing we can experience is the mysterious. It is the source of all true art and science.
Call it a clan, call it a network, call it a tribe, call it a family: Whatever you call it, whoever you are, you need one.
When you reach the end of your rope, tie a knot in it and hang on.
The master has failed more times than the beginner has even tried.
There is no greater agony than bearing an untold story inside you.
You are braver than you believe, stronger than you seem, and smarter than you think.
The geometry of thought is not the same as the grammar of speech.
Spaceship Earth is so extraordinarily well invented that it is self-regenerating.
Sometimes the strength of motherhood is greater than natural laws.
The soup is not the recipe. The soup is what happens when the molecules find each other.
I carry your heart with me (I carry it in my heart).
Hope is the thing with feathers that perches in the soul.
What you seek is seeking you.
The sun shines not on us but in us.
We are all stardust and stories.
The universe began as a thought, not a bang — and thought still holds it open.
Spoons are not weakness. Spoons are honest accounting.
Do not go gentle into that good night. Rage, rage against the dying of the light.
Between stimulus and response there is a space. In that space is our power to choose our response.
The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now.
You are not a drop in the ocean. You are the entire ocean in a drop.
Let yourself be silently drawn by the strange pull of what you really love. It will not lead you astray.
The arc of the moral universe is long, but it bends toward justice.
quotes.attribution: Theodore Parker; Martin Luther King Jr.

quotes.text: We are all the foolish man and the wise man. For no one has avoided a night spent in anguish.
Integrity is doing the right thing, even when no one is watching.
The test of a first-rate intelligence is the ability to hold two opposed ideas in mind at the same time and still retain the ability to function.
When we try to pick out anything by itself, we find it hitched to everything else in the universe.
One child, one teacher, one book, one pen can change the world.
The phosphorus in your bones, the spin in your nucleus — you were always part of the signal.
